title: Within-Stint Curvature And Thermal-Fade Features Instead Of Fixed Life Tables
summary: Recent F1 tyre modeling work emphasizes latent degradation, nonlinear wear, and temperature-window effects rather than a single fixed stint-life threshold.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre modeling work emphasizes latent degradation, nonlinear wear, and temperature-window effects rather than a single fixed stint-life threshold. A pit decision is often triggered by acceleration in loss, not just absolute tyre age, so curvature-style features may outperform more compound-window constants.
implementation_hint: Create leakage-safe within-driver/race/stint features from prior laps only: rolling slope of `LapTime_Delta`, rolling slope of `Cumulative_Degradation`, second-difference/curvature of both, deviation from early-stint baseline, and flags for abrupt fade after stable pace. Interact these with `Compound`, `RaceProgress`, and wet-vs-slick regime.
expected_effect: Should better capture the pre-pit transition from linear wear to thermal cliff, especially for soft/high-energy stints where fixed expected-life features are too coarse.
risk: Sequence features can overfit if derived with any lookahead or if lap ordering is imperfect around pit/reset laps.
